evals.description: Trigger test: activates on bash scripting question
evals.prompt: I'm writing a bash script that deploys our app to production. It runs several commands and I want it to stop immediately if any command fails, rather than continuing with a broken state. What's the right way to do this?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on systemd service question
evals.prompt: I need to run a Python script every night at 3am on a Linux server. Should I use cron or systemd timers? And if systemd, how do I set it up?

evals.description: Core task: write a robust bash script with error handling
evals.prompt: Write me a bash script that accepts a server hostname as an argument, creates a tarball of /etc, and copies it to a backup server. It should fail loudly if anything goes wrong and clean up temp files even if it errors out.

evals.description: Core task: create a systemd service unit for a web app
evals.prompt: I have a Node.js app at /opt/myapp/server.js that should start on boot, restart if it crashes, and run as a dedicated non-root user called 'appuser'. Write the systemd service unit file.
We just provisioned a new Linux VPS that will be internet-facing. Walk me through hardening the SSH configuration to prevent brute-force attacks and unauthorized access.

evals.description: Core task: debug a networking connectivity problem
evals.prompt: My application server can't connect to the PostgreSQL database on a separate host at 10.0.1.20:5432. Both servers are running and I can ping between them. How do I systematically diagnose what's blocking the connection?

evals.description: Core task: set up firewall rules for a web server
evals.prompt: I'm setting up a fresh Ubuntu web server. It needs to serve HTTP and HTTPS to the public, allow SSH only from my office IP (203.0.113.10), and block everything else. Show me how to configure this with ufw.

evals.description: Core task: investigate and free up disk space
evals.prompt: My server's /var partition is at 97% and I need to find and clean up what's consuming the space. Walk me through the investigation and cleanup process.

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about closing SSH session before verifying sshd restart
evals.prompt: I edited /etc/ssh/sshd_config to disable password auth and now I want to restart the SSH service to apply the changes. I'll just run 'sudo systemctl restart sshd'. Is that all I need to do?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about set -e breaking on commands that legitimately return non-zero
evals.prompt: I added 'set -e' to my bash script but now it exits when I run 'grep pattern file' even when grep finding no match is expected and fine. Why is this happening and how do I fix it?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about running services as root
evals.prompt: I'm writing a systemd service unit for my web app. Should I just leave out the User= directive and let it run as root? It's simpler and the app needs to write to /var/log.
My web app can't write to its log directory /var/log/myapp. What file permissions should I set to fix this?
